topic: Someone discovers their friend has been lying about their job
raw_output:
[story 1]

I watched Liam across the coffee shop table, his smile as bright and unblemished as ever. He was recounting his latest project, the details flowing smoothly, a tapestry of impressive achievements. But something felt… off. A tiny, almost imperceptible hitch in his breath every time he mentioned the specifics of his current role at the design firm. I took a slow sip of my lukewarm latte, my gaze lingering on the way his eyes flickered toward the door, a quick dart that seemed too practiced. My jaw tightened almost involuntarily. I knew, deep down, that the narrative he was spinning wasn't quite lining up with the reality I’d pieced together from scattered emails and vague confirmations. When he finally paused, wiping a smudge of sugar from his chin, I didn't offer a congratulatory nod. Instead, I just shifted in my seat, the chair scraping against the floor, and asked, flatly, "So, what exactly does that entail, besides 'innovative problem-solving'?" The air between us thickened instantly.

[story 2]

It started with a misplaced piece of paper. I found it tucked into Liam’s laptop bag—a printout of a company directory, clearly showing a different office location than the one he claimed to be based in. My hands started to tremble slightly as I unfolded the document. Every line seemed to scream contradiction. I spent the next hour poring over the discrepancies, the knot in my stomach tightening with every piece of conflicting data. When Liam finally arrived, radiating an almost forced cheerfulness, I didn't even look up from the screen. "Tell me," I said, my voice carefully level, "how does the downtown location fit into the narrative you’ve been presenting about your career trajectory?" He fidgeted, running a hand through his already messy hair. His eyes darted around the room, avoiding mine. He stammered something about 'reorganization' and 'temporary assignments,' but the sound lacked any real conviction. I leaned back, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, letting the silence stretch until he seemed to break under the weight of my steady, unblinking scrutiny.

[story 3]

I had been tracking Liam’s professional life for weeks, assembling fragments like a puzzle with missing pieces. The evidence was circumstantial—a vague LinkedIn post, a missed phone call at a specific time, the way he avoided discussing certain colleagues. Now, sitting across from him, the pieces slammed together with a jarring finality. I felt a distinct coolness spreading through my core. I kept my expression neutral, a mask of mild curiosity, though inside, a distinct sense of wrongness was churning. "I was just wondering," I began, my tone deliberately light, almost teasing, "if you could elaborate on the travel schedule for the last quarter? It seems quite extensive." Liam’s composure fractured. He started fiddling with the rim of his water glass, his usual easy confidence evaporating. His eyes widened just a fraction, a flicker of panic crossing his face before he managed a weak, rehearsed explanation. I simply watched him, my posture rigid, waiting for the carefully constructed facade to crumble entirely.
